import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.GlobalSections
import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Basic
import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Abelian
import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Linear.LinearFunctor
import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Linear.FunctorCategory
import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Adjunction.Additive
import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Abelian

open CategoryTheory Limits

universe u v u₂ v₂

namespace CategoryTheory.Adjunction
variable {C₁ : Type u} {D₁ : Type u₂} [Category.{v} C₁] [Category.{v₂} D₁]
    [Preadditive C₁] [Preadditive D₁] {F : C₁ ⥤ D₁} {G : D₁ ⥤ C₁} (adj : F ⊣ G)
    (R : Type*) [Semiring R] [CategoryTheory.Linear R C₁] [CategoryTheory.Linear R D₁]

include adj in
lemma left_adjoint_linear [G.Additive] [G.Linear R] : F.Linear R where
  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective <| by
    have h1 : adj.unit.app X ≫ G.map (F.map (r • f)) = (r • f) ≫ adj.unit.app Y := by
      have := adj.unit.naturality (r • f); aesop
    have h2 : adj.unit.app X ≫ G.map (F.map f) = f ≫ adj.unit.app Y := by
      have := adj.unit.naturality f; aesop
    rw [adj.homEquiv_apply, adj.homEquiv_apply, G.map_smul]
    -- Goal: unit ≫ G.map (F.map (r • f)) = unit ≫ (r • G.map (F.map f))
    rw [h1, ← Linear.smul_comp, ← h2]
    -- After substitution we have (r • f) ≫ unit = unit ≫ G.map (r • F.map f)?
    sorry

end CategoryTheory.Adjunction

namespace CategoryTheory.Adjunction
variable {C₁ : Type u} {D₁ : Type u₂} [Category.{v} C₁] [Category.{v₂} D₁]
    [Preadditive C₁] [Preadditive D₁] {F : C₁ ⥤ D₁} {G : D₁ ⥤ C₁} (adj : F ⊣ G)
    (R : Type*) [Semiring R] [CategoryTheory.Linear R C₁] [CategoryTheory.Linear R D₁]

include adj in
example [G.Additive] [G.Linear R] (X Y : C₁) (f : X ⟶ Y) (r : R) :
    F.map (r • f) = r • F.map f := by
  apply (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective

    (R : Type*) [Semiring R] [CategoryTheory.Linear R C₁] [CategoryTheory.Linear R D₁]

include adj in
example [G.Additive] [G.Linear R] (X Y : C₁) (f : X ⟶ Y) (r : R) :
    F.map (r • f) = r • F.map f := by
  apply (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective
  -- LHS = unit ≫ G.map (F.map (r • f)) = (unit naturality) (r • f) ≫ unit_app Y = r • (f ≫ unit)
  -- RHS = unit ≫ G.map (F.map (r • F.map f))?? wait I need to rethink.
  -- adj.homEquiv applied to F.map (r • f) gives unit_app X ≫ G.map (F.map (r • f))
  -- adj.homEquiv applied to r • F.map f gives unit_app X ≫ G.map (r • F.map f)
  -- But the homEquiv is from (F.obj X ⟶ F.obj Y) to (X ⟶ G.obj (F.obj Y))
  -- LHS via naturality: unit ≫ G.map (F.map (r • f)) = (r • f) ≫ unit_app Y = r • (f ≫ unit_app Y)
  -- RHS: unit ≫ G.map (r • F.map f) = unit ≫ r • G.map (F.map f) = r • (unit ≫ G.map (F.map f))
  --      = r • (f ≫ unit_app Y) by naturality on f
  rw [adj.homEquiv_apply, adj.homEquiv_apply]
  have h1 := adj.unit.naturality (r • f)
  have h2 := adj.unit.naturality f
  simp only [Functor.id_obj, Functor.id_map, Functor.comp_obj, Functor.comp_map] at h1 h2

  -- h2: f ≫ unit_app Y = unit_app X ≫ G.map (F.map f)
  rw [← h1, ← h2, G.map_smul, Linear.smul_comp, Linear.comp_smul]

end CategoryTheory.Adjunction

namespace CategoryTheory.Adjunction
variable {C₁ : Type u} {D₁ : Type u₂} [Category.{v} C₁] [Category.{v₂} D₁]

    (R : Type*) [Semiring R] [CategoryTheory.Linear R C₁] [CategoryTheory.Linear R D₁]

include adj in
example [G.Additive] [G.Linear R] (X Y : C₁) (f : X ⟶ Y) (r : R) :

  have h1 : (r • f) ≫ adj.unit.app Y = adj.unit.app X ≫ G.map (F.map (r • f)) :=
    adj.unit.naturality (r • f)
  have h2 : f ≫ adj.unit.app Y = adj.unit.app X ≫ G.map (F.map f) :=
    adj.unit.naturality f
  -- Manually navigate using calc

      = (r • f) ≫ adj.unit.app Y := h1.symm

    simp [adj.homEquiv_unit])

import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK
import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Linear.LinearFunctor
import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Linear.FunctorCategory
import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Adjunction.Additive
import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Abelian

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry

universe u v u₂ v₂

instance Functor.const_additive {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
    [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] : (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Additive where
  map_add := by intros; ext; rfl

instance Functor.const_linear {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
    [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] (R : Type*) [Semiring R] [Linear R D] :
    (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Linear R where
  map_smul := by intros; ext; rfl

namespace CategoryTheory.Adjunction
variable {C₁ : Type u} {D₁ : Type u₂} [Category.{v} C₁] [Category.{v₂} D₁]
    [Preadditive C₁] [Preadditive D₁] {F : C₁ ⥤ D₁} {G : D₁ ⥤ C₁} (adj : F ⊣ G)
    (R : Type*) [Semiring R] [CategoryTheory.Linear R C₁] [CategoryTheory.Linear R D₁]

include adj in
lemma left_adjoint_linear [G.Additive] [G.Linear R] : F.Linear R where
  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective (by
    simp [adj.homEquiv_unit])

include adj in
lemma right_adjoint_linear [F.Additive] [F.Linear R] : G.Linear R where
  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm.injective (by
    simp [adj.homEquiv_counit])

include adj in
noncomputable def homLinearEquiv [F.Additive] [G.Additive] [G.Linear R]
    (X : C₁) (Y : D₁) : (F.obj X ⟶ Y) ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ G.obj Y) :=
  haveI : F.Linear R := adj.left_adjoint_linear R
  { adj.homAddEquiv X Y with
    map_smul' := fun r f => by
      change adj.homEquiv _ _ (r • f) = r • adj.homEquiv _ _ f
      simp [adj.homEquiv_unit] }

end CategoryTheory.Adjunction

-- Goal: the producer instance
noncomputable example
    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
    Module.Finite k
      ((constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
          (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) := by
  -- Step 1: get all the Linear instances
  haveI : (presheafToSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k :=
    (sheafificationAdjunction _ _).left_adjoint_linear k
  haveI : (constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Additive := by
    unfold constantSheaf; infer_instance
  haveI : (Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Additive :=
    (constantSheafΓAdj _ _).right_adjoint_additive
  haveI : (constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k := by
    unfold constantSheaf; infer_instance
  haveI : (Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k :=
    (constantSheafΓAdj _ _).right_adjoint_linear k
  -- Step 2: bridge via the homLinearEquiv to (X ⟶ Sheaf.Γ.obj F)
  -- Step 3: use iter-045 to identify Sheaf.Γ.obj with (toModuleKSheaf C).obj.obj (op ⊤)
  -- Step 4: identify (k ⟶ M) with M.carrier (Hom-from-k iso)
  -- Step 5: transport Module.Finite via iter-044's geometric Stein input
  haveI : Module.Finite k
      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
        (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)) :=
    Scheme.module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper k C
  -- The Hom from ModuleCat.of k k to a ModuleCat M is k-linearly isomorphic to M.
  -- Mathlib: ModuleCat.homEquiv
  let LE1 :=
    ((constantSheafΓAdj _ _).homLinearEquiv k (ModuleCat.of k k) (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
  -- LE1 : ((constantSheaf).obj k ⟶ toModuleKSheaf C) ≃ₗ[k] (k ⟶ Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C))
  -- The codomain is Hom(k, X) for X in ModuleCat k. Need Hom-from-k iso to X.carrier.
  -- ModuleCat.homEquiv: not sure if exists. Let me check.
  sorry

instance Functor.const_linear {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
    [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] (R : Type*) [Semiring R] [Linear R D] :
    (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Linear R where
  map_smul := by intros; ext; rfl

namespace Adjunction
variable {C₁ : Type u} {D₁ : Type u₂} [Category.{v} C₁] [Category.{v₂} D₁]
    [Preadditive C₁] [Preadditive D₁] {F : C₁ ⥤ D₁} {G : D₁ ⥤ C₁} (adj : F ⊣ G)
    (R : Type*) [Semiring R] [CategoryTheory.Linear R C₁] [CategoryTheory.Linear R D₁]

include adj in
lemma left_adjoint_linear [G.Additive] [G.Linear R] : F.Linear R where

    simp [adj.homEquiv_unit])

include adj in
lemma right_adjoint_linear [F.Additive] [F.Linear R] : G.Linear R where
  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm.injective (by
    simp [adj.homEquiv_counit])

include adj in
noncomputable def homLinearEquiv [F.Additive] [G.Additive] [G.Linear R]
    (X : C₁) (Y : D₁) : (F.obj X ⟶ Y) ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ G.obj Y) :=
  haveI : F.Linear R := adj.left_adjoint_linear R
  { adj.homAddEquiv X Y with
    map_smul' := fun r f => by
      change adj.homEquiv _ _ (r • f) = r • adj.homEquiv _ _ f
      simp [adj.homEquiv_unit] }

end Adjunction
end CategoryTheory

import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK

    (ModuleCat.of k k ⟶ M) ≃ₗ[k] M := by

  -- But we need a LinearEquiv.
  -- Strategy: chain ModuleCat.homEquiv with LinearMap.ringLmapEquivSelf
  let e1 : (ModuleCat.of k k ⟶ M) ≃ (k →ₗ[k] M) :=
    ModuleCat.homEquiv (M := ModuleCat.of k k) (N := M)
  let e2 : (k →ₗ[k] M) ≃ₗ[k] M := LinearMap.ringLmapEquivSelf k k M
  -- We need e1 to be a LinearEquiv, not just an Equiv. The Hom group is itself a k-module
  -- via the Linear k structure on ModuleCat k.
  refine LinearEquiv.ofBijective ?_ ?_
  · refine
    { toFun := fun f => f.hom 1

      map_smul' := fun r f => rfl }

    · refine ⟨ModuleCat.ofHom (LinearMap.smulRight (LinearMap.id (R := k) (M := k)) m), ?_⟩
      simp [ModuleCat.ofHom, LinearMap.smulRight]

/- Probe: full producer instance for iter-046+iter-047 bundled -/
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK
import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.Equiv.Basic

namespace CategoryTheory

universe u v u₂ v₂

instance Functor.const_additive {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
    [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] : (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Additive where
  map_add := by intros; ext; rfl

instance Functor.const_linear {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
    [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] (R : Type*) [Semiring R]
    [CategoryTheory.Linear R D] :
    (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Linear R where
  map_smul := by intros; ext; rfl

namespace Adjunction
variable {C₁ : Type u} {D₁ : Type u₂} [Category.{v} C₁] [Category.{v₂} D₁]
    [Preadditive C₁] [Preadditive D₁] {F : C₁ ⥤ D₁} {G : D₁ ⥤ C₁} (adj : F ⊣ G)
    (R : Type*) [Semiring R] [CategoryTheory.Linear R C₁] [CategoryTheory.Linear R D₁]

include adj in
lemma left_adjoint_linear [G.Additive] [G.Linear R] : F.Linear R where
  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective (by simp [adj.homEquiv_unit])

include adj in
lemma right_adjoint_linear [F.Additive] [F.Linear R] : G.Linear R where
  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm.injective (by simp [adj.homEquiv_counit])

include adj in
noncomputable def homLinearEquiv [F.Additive] [G.Additive] [G.Linear R]
    (X : C₁) (Y : D₁) : (F.obj X ⟶ Y) ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ G.obj Y) :=
  haveI : F.Linear R := adj.left_adjoint_linear R
  { adj.homAddEquiv X Y with
    map_smul' := fun r f => by
      change adj.homEquiv _ _ (r • f) = r • adj.homEquiv _ _ f
      simp [adj.homEquiv_unit] }

end Adjunction
end CategoryTheory

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
universe u v

noncomputable def constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv
    (k : Type u) [Field k] {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C]
    (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
    (X : ModuleCat.{u} k) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
    ((constantSheaf J _).obj X ⟶ F) ≃ₗ[k] (X ⟶ (Sheaf.Γ J _).obj F) :=
  haveI : (presheafToSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k :=
    (sheafificationAdjunction J _).left_adjoint_linear k
  haveI : (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Additive := by
    unfold constantSheaf; infer_instance
  haveI : (Sheaf.Γ J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Additive :=
    (constantSheafΓAdj J _).right_adjoint_additive
  haveI : (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k := by
    unfold constantSheaf; infer_instance
  haveI : (Sheaf.Γ J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k :=
    (constantSheafΓAdj J _).right_adjoint_linear k
  (constantSheafΓAdj J _).homLinearEquiv k X F

/-- Hom-from-k LinearEquiv. -/
noncomputable def homFromOne_linearEquiv (k : Type u) [Field k] (M : ModuleCat.{u} k) :
    (ModuleCat.of k k ⟶ M) ≃ₗ[k] M :=
  (ModuleCat.homLinearEquiv (M := ModuleCat.of k k) (N := M) (S := k)).trans
    (LinearMap.ringLmapEquivSelf k k M)

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

-- Producer instance attempt
noncomputable instance test_producer
    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
    Scheme.IsHModuleHomFinite k C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) where
  module_finite_hom := by
    haveI := Scheme.module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper k C
    -- LinearEquiv chain:
    let LE1 := Scheme.constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv k
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.of k k) (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)
    -- LE1: (constantSheaf k → toModuleKSheaf C) ≃ₗ[k] (k → Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C))
    let LE2 := Scheme.homFromOne_linearEquiv k
      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
        (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
    -- LE2: (k → Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C)) ≃ₗ[k] Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C)
    -- Compose: LE1.trans LE2 : (constantSheaf k → toModuleKSheaf C) ≃ₗ[k] Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C)
    -- Transport Module.Finite from Sheaf.Γ.obj (which we have via haveI) to source
    exact Module.Finite.equiv (LE1.trans LE2).symm

# Project Progress

## Current Stage
prover  (iteration 046 — Phase A step 6 *Path 2* / Serre-finiteness scaffolding **producer instance** for the iter-043 wholespace H⁰ Hom-finiteness carrier on the curve sheaf: append eight declarations to `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` to produce `IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` end-to-end. Five general Mathlib gap-fills go into a new `namespace CategoryTheory` block at the file head: (1) `instance Functor.const_additive`, (2) `instance Functor.const_linear`, (3) `lemma Adjunction.left_adjoint_linear`, (4) `lemma Adjunction.right_adjoint_linear`, (5) `noncomputable def Adjunction.homLinearEquiv` (linear lifting of Mathlib's `Adjunction.homAddEquiv`). Three project-local applied declarations go inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme` after iter-045's `module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper`: (6) `noncomputable def constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv` — applied LinearEquiv from `constantSheafΓAdj` for `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`; (7) `noncomputable def homFromOne_linearEquiv` — Hom-from-`k` upgrade `(ModuleCat.of k k ⟶ M) ≃ₗ[k] M`; (8) `noncomputable instance instIsHModuleHomFinite_toModuleKSheaf` — **the producer instance**, completing the four-step chain (constantSheafΓAdj LinearEquiv → iter-045 SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top → Hom-from-k upgrade → iter-044 Module.Finite transport via Module.Finite.equiv). Iter-045 closure verified (this pass): two declarations land cleanly at L580–633; sorry count `9 → 9`; file LOC `615 → 670` (+55, within plan estimate band of +30–50 — slight overage from multi-paragraph load-bearing docstrings). Plan-agent `lean_run_code` probe (this pass) confirms all eight iter-046 declarations compose cleanly `{success: true, diagnostics: []}` end-to-end and the producer instance has kernel-only axioms `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`. Iter-046 sorry trajectory `9 → 9` (no transient — bodies probe-confirmed). Iter-046 closes steps (1)+(3)+(4) of the iter-044 four-step producer-instance plan; combined with iter-045's step (2) this completes the **full producer chain** for `IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`. Once landed, iter-043's `module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite_curve` consumer fires automatically on `Module.Finite k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0)` queries. Iter-047+ produces the parallel carrier instance `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` (multi-iteration; via Čech-vs-derived comparison + affine Čech vanishing), and iter-048+ consumes via the LES + iter-035 corner-bridge to derive `Module.Finite k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1)` (Step 5 — the genus carrier).

## Stages
- [x] init
- [x] autoformalize
- [ ] prover
- [ ] polish

## Big-picture context

This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.

`STRATEGY.md` describes the multi-phase build-out path (Phases A–E). Iter-005/006/007 closed Phase A steps 1–5 + polish. Iter-008 closed Track 2 (étale sheafification of `PicardFunctorAb`). Iter-009 closed the Phase A step 5/6 bridge (`HModule` infrastructure for `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology). Iter-010 closed the Phase A step 6 *algebraic preliminary* (`HModule_zero_linearEquiv`). Iter-011 closed the **first protected sorry** (`AlgebraicGeometry.genus`). Iter-012 opened Path-2 Čech scaffold (`Scheme.cechCochain_OC`, `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC`). Iter-014/015 closed the Mayer-Vietoris prerequisite `Scheme.HModule'` and its `H⁰` bridge. Iter-016 → iter-026 closed the **complete Mayer-Vietoris LES** for the `ModuleCat k` flavor, mirroring Mathlib `MayerVietoris.lean` L43–149 line-by-line. Iter-027 split the cohort verbatim into a new file `Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`. Iter-028 opened **Serre-finiteness scaffolding**: a bundled 2-affine MV square `Scheme.AffineCoverMVSquare` together with the accessor `toMayerVietorisSquare`. Iter-029 → iter-030 closed cover-corner identifications + sheaf-parameterised + curve-specialised LES. Iter-031 → iter-034 closed the cover-totality bridge `HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv : HModule' k F n T ≃ₗ[k] HModule k F n` for terminal `T` (Step 3). Iter-035 closed Step 3.5 (the corner-bridge specialisation `AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_X₄_linearEquiv`). Iter-036 appended the immediate finrank corollary `finrank_HModule_eq_HModule'_X₄` (+ `_curve` companion). Iter-037 appended the parallel `Module.Finite` transport companion `module_finite_HModule_of_HModule'_X₄` (+ `_curve` companion). Iter-038 appended the **degree-zero `Module.Finite` transport companions** for the iter-010 / iter-015 H⁰ bridges (`Scheme.module_finite_HModule_zero` + `_HModule'_zero`). Iter-039 appended the **curve specialisations** of iter-038's H⁰ transports. Iter-040 closed the **affine cohomology vanishing carrier predicate** `class Scheme.IsAffineHModuleVanishing` together with its immediate consumer. Iter-041 appended the (mis-designed) "all affines" H⁰ Hom finiteness carrier `class Scheme.IsAffineHModuleHomFinite` + immediate consumer (carrier dead — not instantiable on a proper $k$-curve). Iter-042 appended the combined affine-corner consumer `module_finite_HModule'_of_affine` + curve specialisation. Iter-043 appended the **wholespace** corrective: `class Scheme.IsHModuleHomFinite` capturing only the **global** Hom group (`((constantSheaf).obj _ ⟶ F)`, morally $\Gamma(C, F)$, finite via Stein on proper geometrically connected curves) + immediate `Module.Finite` consumer + curve specialisation. Iter-044 appended the **geometric Stein input** `Scheme.module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper : [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] → Module.Finite k Γ(C, ⊤)` via Mathlib's `finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed`. Iter-045 appended the **global-sections evaluation iso** `Scheme.SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top` lifting `Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections` to a `LinearEquiv` + immediate consumer `module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper`. Iter-046 (this iteration) appends **eight declarations** including the **producer instance** `instIsHModuleHomFinite_toModuleKSheaf` — completing the producer chain end-to-end. After iter-046, iter-047+ produces the parallel carrier instance `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` (multi-iteration; via Čech-vs-derived comparison + affine Čech vanishing), and iter-048+ consumes via the LES to derive the eventual `Module.Finite k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1)` — the genus carrier.

## Iteration 046 plan-agent summary (this iteration)

**Iter-045 verification.** Plan-agent verification (this pass) confirms iter-045 closure landed cleanly:

- **Sorry count**: `${LEAN4_PYTHON_BIN:-python3} "$LEAN4_SCRIPTS/sorry_analyzer.py" AlgebraicJacobian/ --format=summary` returns `9 total across 3 file(s)` (5 `Jacobian.lean` + 3 `AbelJacobi.lean` + 1 `Picard/Functor.lean`). Pre-iter-045 `9`, post-iter-045 `9`, target post-iter-046 prover `9`.
- **File diagnostics**: `lean_diagnostic_messages` on `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` returns `{success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []}`. Zero warnings, zero errors.
- **File LOC**: `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` 670 LOC (iter-045 added 55 LOC = 615 → 670; +55 vs +30-50 plan estimate band — slight overage from multi-paragraph load-bearing docstrings).
- **Iter-045 declarations placed correctly**: `noncomputable def SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top` at L597; `theorem module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper` at L622; namespace block closes at L635.
- **`Genus.lean` preserved**: still 0 sorries; iter-011 first-protected closure intact.
- **`archon-protected.yaml`**: `git diff archon-protected.yaml` returns empty. Unchanged.
- **No new axioms**: no new `axiom` declarations in `AlgebraicJacobian/`.
- **Iter-045 axiom checks**:
  - `lean_verify AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top` → `{axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound], warnings: []}` — kernel-only.
  - `lean_verify AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper` → `{axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound], warnings: []}` — kernel-only.

**Iter-046 work scope.** With iter-045's evaluation iso in scope, the iter-044 four-step producer-instance plan reduces to closing steps (1), (3), and (4) — the linearised constant-sheaf-Γ adjunction homEquiv (the substantive Mathlib gap-fill), the Hom-from-`k` upgrade, and the final `Module.Finite.equiv` transport. Plan-agent end-to-end probe (this pass; via `lean_run_code`) confirms all three steps land cleanly together with the producer instance, with **kernel-only axioms** for every declaration in the chain. **Iter-046 therefore collapses the originally-multi-iteration step (1) plus iter-047's planned producer assembly into a single iteration**.

**Mathlib re-probe (this pass).**

1. **`Functor.Linear` exists** in `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Linear/LinearFunctor.lean` (transitively in scope): the `Prop`-valued class for `R`-linear functors between `Linear R`-enriched categories. Composition is automatic via `Functor.Linear.instLinearComp`.

2. **`Adjunction.homAddEquiv` exists** in `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Adjunction/Additive.lean` (transitively in scope): for `adj : F ⊣ G` between preadditive categories with `[F.Additive]`, lifts the bare `homEquiv` to an additive equivalence `(F.obj X ⟶ Y) ≃+ (X ⟶ G.obj Y)`. Mathlib does **not** have a `LinearEquiv` analogue.

3. **`Adjunction.left_adjoint_additive`** + **`right_adjoint_additive`** exist (same file) — propagating `Additive` across an adjunction. Mathlib does **not** have `Linear` analogues.

4. **`Adjunction.constantSheafΓAdj`** exists in `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/GlobalSections.lean` (transitively in scope): `constantSheaf J A ⊣ Sheaf.Γ J A` whenever `[HasWeakSheafify J A] [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J A]`.

5. **`presheafToSheaf_additive`** exists in `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Abelian.lean` (transitively in scope via `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Abelian`): `(presheafToSheaf J D).Additive` for `[Abelian D] [HasSheafify J D]`. Mathlib does **not** have a `Linear` analogue, but it follows from the iter-046 gap-fill `Adjunction.left_adjoint_linear` applied to `sheafificationAdjunction`.

6. **`Functor.const`** has neither an `Additive` nor a `Linear` instance in Mathlib (project-local instance needed; one-liner via `intros; ext; rfl`).

7. **`ModuleCat.homLinearEquiv`** exists in `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Basic.lean` — `(M ⟶ N) ≃ₗ[S] (↑M →ₗ[R] ↑N)` for any commuting scalar action `S` on `↑N`. Combined with **`LinearMap.ringLmapEquivSelf`** (`Mathlib/Algebra/Module/Equiv/Basic.lean`) which gives `(R →ₗ[R] M) ≃ₗ[S] M`, yields the Hom-from-`k` upgrade `(ModuleCat.of k k ⟶ M) ≃ₗ[k] M` as a one-liner.

Plan-agent probes the bodies in detail:

1. **Probe-confirmed body (declaration 1: `Functor.const_additive`)** (this pass; tested via `lean_run_code`):
   ```lean
   instance Functor.const_additive {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
       [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] : (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Additive where
     map_add := by intros; ext; rfl
   ```

2. **Probe-confirmed body (declaration 2: `Functor.const_linear`)** (this pass):
   ```lean
   instance Functor.const_linear {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
       [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] (R : Type*) [Semiring R]
       [CategoryTheory.Linear R D] :
       (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Linear R where
     map_smul := by intros; ext; rfl
   ```
   The `[CategoryTheory.Linear R D]` qualification is load-bearing — bare `[Linear R D]` is parsed as `Linear` of a functor, not the category enrichment.

3. **Probe-confirmed bodies (declarations 3-4: `Adjunction.left_adjoint_linear` + `right_adjoint_linear`)** (this pass):
   ```lean

/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): `Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D` is additive when `D`

instance Functor.const_additive {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
    [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] : (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Additive where
  map_add := by intros; ext; rfl

/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): `Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear when
`D` is `Linear R`-enriched. The `[CategoryTheory.Linear R D]` qualification is
load-bearing — bare `[Linear R D]` would be parsed as `Linear` of a functor. -/
instance Functor.const_linear {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
    [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] (R : Type*) [Semiring R]
    [CategoryTheory.Linear R D] :
    (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Linear R where
  map_smul := by intros; ext; rfl

namespace Adjunction

variable {C₁ : Type u} {D₁ : Type u₂} [Category.{v} C₁] [Category.{v₂} D₁]
    [Preadditive C₁] [Preadditive D₁] {F : C₁ ⥤ D₁} {G : D₁ ⥤ C₁} (adj : F ⊣ G)
    (R : Type*) [Semiring R] [CategoryTheory.Linear R C₁] [CategoryTheory.Linear R D₁]

/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): if `G : D ⥤ C` is `R`-linear and `adj : F ⊣ G`,
then `F : C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear. Mirrors Mathlib's `left_adjoint_additive`; the
`simp` call closes via unit naturality + the `Linear.smul_comp` / `Linear.comp_smul`
default simp lemmas. -/
include adj in
lemma left_adjoint_linear [G.Additive] [G.Linear R] : F.Linear R where
  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective (by
    simp [adj.homEquiv_unit])

/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): if `F : C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear and `adj : F ⊣ G`,
then `G : D ⥤ C` is `R`-linear. The dual of `left_adjoint_linear`. -/
include adj in
lemma right_adjoint_linear [F.Additive] [F.Linear R] : G.Linear R where
  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm.injective (by
    simp [adj.homEquiv_counit])

`change` is load-bearing: it exposes the underlying `homEquiv` form, allowing
`simp [homEquiv_unit]` to close via the same naturality argument as in
`left_adjoint_linear`. -/
include adj in

    (X : C₁) (Y : D₁) : (F.obj X ⟶ Y) ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ G.obj Y) :=

  { adj.homAddEquiv X Y with
    map_smul' := fun r f => by
      change adj.homEquiv _ _ (r • f) = r • adj.homEquiv _ _ f
```

```lean
/-- Iter-046: applied LinearEquiv from the constant-sheaf-Γ adjunction. For any
sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)` and `X : ModuleCat k`, gives a `k`-LinearEquiv
between the Hom group `((constantSheaf).obj X ⟶ F)` (sheaf morphisms from the
constant sheaf at `X`) and `(X ⟶ (Sheaf.Γ).obj F)` (module morphisms into the
global sections of `F`).

Built from `(constantSheafΓAdj).homLinearEquiv` (iter-046 Mathlib gap-fill).
The five `haveI` lines establish the typeclass scaffolding required to invoke
the gap-fill: `presheafToSheaf` Linear (via `sheafificationAdjunction`),
`constantSheaf` Additive + Linear (via composition), and `Sheaf.Γ` Additive +
Linear (via the `right_adjoint_*` propagators along `constantSheafΓAdj`).

Used in iter-046's producer instance `instIsHModuleHomFinite_toModuleKSheaf`
to bridge from `Sheaf.Γ.obj`-finiteness (iter-045) to Hom-from-constantSheaf-
finiteness (the `IsHModuleHomFinite` carrier). -/
noncomputable def constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv
    (k : Type u) [Field k] {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C]
    (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
    (X : ModuleCat.{u} k) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
    ((constantSheaf J _).obj X ⟶ F) ≃ₗ[k] (X ⟶ (Sheaf.Γ J _).obj F) :=
  haveI : (presheafToSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k :=
    (sheafificationAdjunction J _).left_adjoint_linear k
  haveI : (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Additive := by
    unfold constantSheaf; infer_instance
  haveI : (Sheaf.Γ J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Additive :=
    (constantSheafΓAdj J _).right_adjoint_additive
  haveI : (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k := by
    unfold constantSheaf; infer_instance
  haveI : (Sheaf.Γ J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k :=
    (constantSheafΓAdj J _).right_adjoint_linear k
  (constantSheafΓAdj J _).homLinearEquiv k X F

/-- Iter-046: Hom-from-`k` upgrade. The Hom group `(ModuleCat.of k k ⟶ M)` for
`M : ModuleCat k` is canonically `k`-LinearEquivalent to `M` via `f ↦ f 1`.
Direct one-liner combining `ModuleCat.homLinearEquiv` (Mathlib's
LinearEquiv-version of the underlying-LinearMap correspondence) with
`LinearMap.ringLmapEquivSelf` (the standard `(R →ₗ[R] M) ≃ₗ[S] M` evaluation). -/
noncomputable def homFromOne_linearEquiv (k : Type u) [Field k] (M : ModuleCat.{u} k) :
    (ModuleCat.of k k ⟶ M) ≃ₗ[k] M :=
  (ModuleCat.homLinearEquiv (M := ModuleCat.of k k) (N := M) (S := k)).trans
    (LinearMap.ringLmapEquivSelf k k M)

/-- Iter-046: **the producer instance** for `IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`
on a proper integral `Spec k`-scheme `C`. Closes the four-step chain:

  (1) `constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv` (iter-046 step 1) bridges the Hom group
      `((constantSheaf).obj k ⟶ toModuleKSheaf C)` to `(k ⟶ Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C))`.
  (2) `homFromOne_linearEquiv` (iter-046 step 3) identifies `(k ⟶ M)` with `M`
      as `k`-modules.
  (3) Combined LinearEquiv from (1)+(2): `((constantSheaf).obj k ⟶ toModuleKSheaf C)
      ≃ₗ[k] Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C)`.
  (4) `module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper` (iter-045 step 2) provides
      `Module.Finite k (Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C))` from `[IsIntegral C.left]
      [IsProper C.hom]` (iter-044 geometric Stein input).
  (5) Transport via `Module.Finite.equiv (.symm)` of the combined LinearEquiv.

Once landed, iter-043's curve consumer `module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite_curve`
fires automatically on `Module.Finite k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0)` queries,
closing the H⁰ side of the genus-finrank Module.Finite ladder.

Marked `instance` (not `theorem`) — this is a *producer* of a typeclass instance,
to be picked up by typeclass synthesis when the consumer asks for
`IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`. Hypotheses `[IsIntegral C.left]`,
`[IsProper C.hom]` are class arguments propagated via instance synthesis from
the use site (where `C` is concretely a proper integral `Spec k`-scheme, e.g.\ a
smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve). -/
noncomputable instance instIsHModuleHomFinite_toModuleKSheaf
    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
    IsHModuleHomFinite k C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) where
  module_finite_hom := by
    haveI := Scheme.module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper k C
    let LE1 := constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv k
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.of k k)
      (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)
    let LE2 := homFromOne_linearEquiv k
      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
        (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
    exact Module.Finite.equiv (LE1.trans LE2).symm
```

For `Opens.grothendieckTopology X`, the terminal is `⊤` (top open in any preorder with a top element, witnessed by `Preorder.isTerminalTop`). Combined with `NatIso.app F` and `Iso.toLinearEquiv` (categorical iso in `ModuleCat k` → `k`-LinearEquiv on carriers), this gives a one-line `LinearEquiv`.

   ```lean
   noncomputable def SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top
       (k : Type u) [Field k] {X : TopCat.{u}}
       (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
       (Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj F
         ≃ₗ[k] F.obj.obj (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)) :=
     ((Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
         (ModuleCat.{u} k) (T := ⊤) (Preorder.isTerminalTop _)).app F).toLinearEquiv
   ```

   ```lean
   theorem module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper
       (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
       [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
       Module.Finite k
         ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
           (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)) := by
     haveI : Module.Finite k
         ((Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C).obj.obj
           (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)) : ModuleCat k) :=
   ```
